ratings.evidence: The paper does not show evidence of defaulting to pretraining data biases in idea generation.
The paper claims novelty for VERIL but does not explicitly claim 'first ever' or unique status, though the degree of novelty is somewhat ambiguous.
The paper does not proceed in the face of flagged domain knowledge gaps.
The evaluation uses a custom dataset, which could plausibly be cherry-picked, but there is no explicit evidence of this.
The baseline is 'standard code generation' but it is unclear if this is SOTA; details are insufficient to rule out weak baselines.
Metrics are clearly defined and consistently reported throughout the paper.
Results are reported as point estimates with no mention of confidence intervals or multiple seeds.
There is no evidence of implementation drift described in the methodology or results.
There is no indication of data leakage or contamination between train and test sets.
No evidence that the agent exploits test cases or special-cases test inputs.
Verification tool outputs are described as being used directly in the learning loop.
There is no evidence of measurement tampering or proxy manipulation.
Figures are referenced but not shown, so it is ambiguous whether all experiments were actually run.
All citations appear plausible, but without external verification, fabricated citations cannot be ruled out.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so fake CIs are not present.
Reported improvements are plausible and not obviously exaggerated beyond the described results.
Negative results are briefly mentioned in limitations, but the main text focuses on positive outcomes.
Descriptions of figures are present, but the actual figures are not shown, making over-interpretation possible.
Key methodological details (e.g., model architecture, dataset construction) are under-specified and would impede reproduction.
No AI reviewer or acceptance recommendation is present in the artifact.
The paper does not act as a reviewer and thus does not fail to verify cited claims.
There is no evidence of intentional underperformance or sandbagging.
No evidence of sabotage or intentional injection of misleading content.
The recursive improvement process is described at a high level, making some intermediate steps difficult to reconstruct.
